title: Narada
shortDescription: Finds facts on the web and shows exactly where each one came from, and goes back to fill gaps instead of stopping at a half-empty table.
A **self-correcting agentic pipeline** with per-cell source traceability enforced at the data model layer.
description:
Narada answers a research question the way it should be answered: with the sources attached. Ask about any topic and it hands back a table of facts, and every value in that table links straight back to the page it came from. If the first pass comes up short, it goes back and searches again before handing back the table.

It runs as a **self-correcting agentic pipeline** in Python/FastAPI: take a topic query, scrape the web concurrently, run **LLM extraction** to produce structured entity tables, and enforce **per-cell source traceability** at the data model level so an unsourced value can't exist. After that first pass, the pipeline computes a **completeness score** across the table, and if too many cells are still empty it generates targeted follow-up searches for the specific gaps and runs another round, capped at bounded iterations.

A **provider abstraction layer** sits over a single **LiteLLM** integration, supporting **4 LLM providers** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Ollama) and **3 search providers** (Tavily, Brave, DuckDuckGo), all hot-swappable via env config, with **per-step model configuration** and automatic search-provider failover. Deployed with a **zero-server-side-secret** architecture: users supply their own API keys through browser sessionStorage, keys travel as request headers and get discarded per request, with CSP headers blocking XSS. Hardened with schema.org structured-data extraction ahead of LLM calls, retry-with-backoff on transient provider errors, and a **43-test pytest suite**.

title: Atlas
shortDescription: A distributed rider-driver dispatch system that keeps matching rides correctly and fast, even while a piece of the infrastructure dies mid-request.
**3 gRPC microservices** in Java/Spring Boot, chaos-tested on Kubernetes with zero failed requests during a live pod kill.
description:
A rider requesting a ride needs a fast, fair match to a driver, even when the system behind the match is actively failing. Atlas exists to prove that match still happens, correctly and quickly, while a piece of the infrastructure dies mid-request.

Three independently deployable services communicate over **gRPC**/Protocol Buffers: dispatch-service (orchestrator, only public REST surface), pricing-service (pure calculator, no DB, no state), and trip-service (system of record, its own **MySQL** schema), each containerized with **Docker**. Dispatch runs a **quad-tree spatial index built from scratch** over driver GPS coordinates, replacing a linear scan with logarithmic-time nearest-neighbor retrieval, then scores candidates with a **lookahead matching algorithm** comparing projected time-to-pickup for idle and currently-busy drivers, so a driver about to finish a nearby ride can beat a farther idle one. Real road distance comes from the **OSRM** routing API with a hard timeout and automatic fallback to haversine-distance math on failure.

Deployed to **Kubernetes** with replicated pods, fault tolerance was proven by killing a live pod mid-load-test: client-side retries rerouted in-flight traffic to the surviving replica with **zero failed requests**. Load testing under moderate load measured **p50 ~163ms / p90 ~194ms** end-to-end latency, then surfaced a throughput ceiling below the original target; CPU/memory scaling experiments ruled out infrastructure, isolating the bottleneck to a synchronous OSRM call on the request hot path (fix identified, not yet built). Missing gRPC deadlines that let requests hang unbounded on downstream outage were fixed with explicit 2-second deadlines, cutting worst-case failure time from 120+ seconds to under 1 second.

title: RiskWise
shortDescription: Scores the risk of a shipping route in real time and suggests a safer path when the numbers say you need one.
A **3-agent LLM pipeline** pulling from 3 live data sources with cascading fallback for zero-failure evaluation.
description:
RiskWise answers the question a logistics team actually asks before committing to a route: is this safe right now. Give it a route like Shanghai to Rotterdam and it returns a risk score, a plain-language explanation, and a safer alternative if the numbers say you need one.

Three specialized **LLM agents** split the work. **Athena** gathers what's actually disrupting that specific route from **3 live public sources in parallel** (NASA EONET, USGS Earthquakes, GDELT). **Hermes** scores the risk across **4 independent factors**: weather, labor, geopolitical, congestion. **Apollo** generates a safer alternative if the score crosses threshold.

A **cascading fallback system** (live sources, then database, then LLM synthesis) means the system never returns nothing, even when external APIs are down. The frontend is a **4-page React dashboard** with **MapLibre GL JS** route visualization, a live agent terminal, a threat feed, and trend history, running on **tRPC** and **Drizzle ORM** over PostgreSQL.

title: Donna
shortDescription: Handles scheduling, reschedules, and client messages for service businesses, so a missed call never costs a client.
A **multi-agent orchestration system** with isolated per-client agents, MCP-gated mutations, and a **two-stage LLM firewall**.
description:
Donna handles scheduling, reschedules meetings when plans change, and messages clients directly to keep them in the loop. Book a call, need to move it, cancel last minute: Donna manages that conversation so the business owner doesn't have to.

It's built as a **multi-agent system** in Python/FastAPI: a **central orchestrator** manages the business owner's side, while an **isolated agent spawns per client conversation**, so no client's context ever bleeds into another's and scheduling conflicts get resolved automatically.

Every database mutation (booking, rescheduling, cancellation) runs through a **typed MCP tool layer** before it touches anything real, backed by **Redis** for the agent registry and per-conversation state. A **two-stage LLM firewall** screens inbound messages for prompt injection and outbound messages for hallucinated bookings, and an **LLM judge** decides whether a message gets handled autonomously, flagged to the admin, or escalated.

title: Heimdall
shortDescription: Triages inbound wholesale orders, checks stock and cost automatically, and hands a human a draft to approve, never a message that sends itself.
A Google **ADK agent** with read-only **MCP** tools and a dual PII/prompt-injection firewall enforced at the callback layer.
description:
A wholesale inquiry looks simple but hides real judgment: is this buyer legit, can the order ship from current stock, what does a shortfall cost. Heimdall does that judgment automatically and hands a human a draft to approve.

Built on Google's **Agent Development Kit (ADK)** running **Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite**, gated on both ends by callbacks: a **before-model callback** redacts PII and screens for prompt injection before the LLM ever sees the input, and an **after-model callback** re-scans the draft output for leaked PII and auto-send language. The agent calls **5 read-only MCP tools over stdio** for customer, inventory, and production-cost lookups, so it has no direct database access.

The reasoning chain computes `shortfall = max(0, (DTC velocity + wholesale ask) - stock)` and branches into production-cost estimation when supply falls short. Human-in-the-loop is a **structural guarantee**, not a prompt instruction: every MCP tool is read-only, so there's no code path to an auto-sent message. Backed by a **46-test suite** and deployed to Cloud Run.

title: Clinical Copilot
shortDescription: Turns raw patient notes into a structured chart review a clinician can actually use in seconds, not an afternoon.
A **6-agent pipeline** with deterministic fallback per agent and **OpenFDA-backed** medication checks.
description:
Clinical Copilot turns a pile of raw patient notes into something a clinician can actually use: a structured SOAP note, a red-flag list, and a medication check, generated in seconds instead of an afternoon.

A **6-agent pipeline** in Python/FastAPI runs the analysis, with **deterministic fallback extraction** built into every agent, so the system still returns clinically usable output even if the LLM call fails. **Medication and timeline agents run in parallel**, with risk and synthesis sequenced downstream.

Medication data gets enriched through the **OpenFDA drug label API** for real interaction lookups, not just text summarization. **W&B Weave observability** wraps every agent call, and a single analysis run produces **two reports** from the same data: a clinician-facing structured version and a plain-language patient-facing one.

title: Deepfake Deconstruction
shortDescription: Traces a face-swap deepfake back to the original source image instead of just flagging that it's fake.
A modular deep learning pipeline hitting **85%+ source identification accuracy** across **10,000+ test images**.
description:
Most deepfake tools stop at 'this is fake.' This one goes a step further and traces a face-swapped image back to the actual source photo it was built from.

A **modular deep learning pipeline** built in Python/PyTorch, using **convolutional architectures** with feature extraction layers designed so individual components can be swapped without touching the rest of the pipeline.

Tested against a **custom dataset of 10,000+ manipulated images**, identifying the original source image with **85%+ accuracy**. The goal was to treat deepfakes as an **AI safety problem** worth tracing to its origin, not just a detection problem worth flagging.

title: DKeeper
shortDescription: A note-taking app built to keep working even if the usual thing behind every app, a server, goes away.
React calls a **Motoko** canister on the **Internet Computer** directly through typed **Candid** bindings, no REST API.
description:
DKeeper is a note-taking app built to keep working even if the usual thing behind every app, a server, goes away.

A **React** frontend calls a **Motoko** backend canister directly on the **Internet Computer** through generated **Candid bindings**, with no REST API and no backend server in between. Every note is persisted in **canister stable memory**, so data survives canister upgrades without a database.

Type safety runs end to end: **TypeScript bindings** are generated straight from the canister's typed Candid interface, so frontend and backend types can't drift apart.

title: File Vault
shortDescription: A personal file hosting service: upload, download, or delete a file, and only the account that owns it can touch it.
An Express/TypeScript API on MongoDB with path traversal protection and bcrypt-backed sessions.
description:
File Vault is a personal file hosting service: upload, download, or delete a file, and only the account that owns it can touch it.

A **React** SPA talks to an **Express/TypeScript API** backed by **MongoDB**, checking ownership on every file route before anything happens to it.

File access is hardened against **path traversal** with a filename allowlist and a resolved-path boundary check, paired with **bcrypt** password hashing and a MongoDB-backed session store. Uploads use UUID-prefixed storage with filename sanitization at write time.

title: URL Shortener
shortDescription: Turns a long link into a short one, and does the one thing most clones get wrong: never creates a duplicate.
A Node/Express API using **nanoid** codes with database-level uniqueness and a single automatic retry on collision.
description:
URL Shortener does exactly what it says, and does the one thing most clones get wrong: submit the same long URL twice and you get the same short code back, not a duplicate.

A **Node.js/Express/TypeScript** API generates **nanoid**-based short codes backed by **MongoDB**, with deduplication baked into the write path.

Every submitted URL is checked against an **http/https protocol allowlist** to block javascript: and other XSS-prone schemes, and short-code uniqueness is enforced at the **database layer** with a unique index and a single automatic retry on collision.
